phase3_findings.description: annotation || true swallows describe failure -> unlogged splice (promoted from iter-1 shadow)
review-tier source=ok but file vanished -> silent baseline (promoted)
review-tier settings classification/routing inline shell only grep-pinned (extract-into-helper convention)

fix_decisions.claim_text: review-tier settings ok/degraded/absent classification + compose-routing is inline branch-selecting shell, only grep-pinned; convention says extract into a scripts/*.sh helper the suite can drive per-arm
fix_decisions.evidence: over-grade shape 3 (uncorroborated single-source from empirical over-grader pr-test-analyzer; not raised by any other of the 5 shadow agents, no Phase-2 FAIL). Technical evaluation: the finding is REAL (the classification IS inline and grep-only pinned) but (a) the current behavior is verified-correct by all 5 reviewers across two passes; (b) the security-load-bearing invariant (review tier never reads PR-head settings) already has a behavioral NEGATIVE pin; (c) the classification is git-materialization-coupled (git show / git cat-file -e against FETCH_HEAD), so a faithful extraction is a scope-expanding test-architecture refactor (a new scripts/classify-compose-read.sh + git_sandbox tests) better tracked as its own follow-up issue than grown unboundedly inside this PR. Recorded as a DEFERRED follow-up, not auto-fixed. Not a self-contradicting-diff carve-out (it is a coverage/testability finding, not a false claim), so it is deferrable.

fix_decisions.claim_text: duplicate breadcrumb text on a wholly-degraded settings file
fix_decisions.evidence: cosmetic (the defect is still surfaced, just doubled); Suggestion, below fix threshold. Optional dedupe not worth a fix-loop iteration.

skipped_checks.reason: working tree dirty (grades committed HEAD) — resolves post-commit / runs in CI

stale_prose_check.rows: no STALE rows at post-iter-2 HEAD

test_first_gate.decision: coverage-add for existing behavior (read-error arm + 'missing' fallback)
test_first_gate.command: direct helper invocation with directory path and no-source-kind fixture
test_first_gate.evidence: directory path -> 'could not be read ([Errno 21] Is a directory)' and NOT 'not valid JSON'; no-source-kind -> 'non-github source kind missing'. Both asserted breadcrumbs confirmed live.

test_first_gate.decision: behavioral pins for new inline arms (renderer-failure fallback + vanished-settings)
test_first_gate.command: grep-pins (inline workflow shell not helper-extractable) + negative pin that '|| true' on the COMPOSE call is gone
test_first_gate.evidence: suite 7675 passed incl. the new pins; negative pin correctly does not match the legitimate bash "$HELPER" ... || true lines

sweep_evidence.note: fix delta = workflow-shell arms + doc + test coverage; §2.3 always-on coupled-mirror sweep applied — the annotation-fallback change is mirrored across all three tiers (verified byte-consistent), the arm-ref comment fix is single-site, and the run.sh pins cover the new arms

phase3_findings.description: SECOND (line-wrapped) 'arm 2' cross-reference at devflow-runner.yml baseprovision comment — a degraded-no-entries file routes to arm 3; diff-added comment false against HEAD (documented_falsehood / self-contradicting diff). MISSED in iter-2 because the phrase wraps across a #-prefixed continuation, defeating line/ws-normalized grep.
grep-derived helper-trust discriminator ('name':'devflow') is a guard-class-2 selection via a non-preflight tool
computed baseline output has no in-step fallback — a mid-step mktemp abort drops all plugins incl devflow@ (fails LOUD)
bash $HELPER || true can swallow a stderr-less exec failure without an audit line
inline workflow compose-routing branch-selection only grep-pinned (overlap with iter-2 deferred; reviewer: 'not a blocker, accepted limitation')
undriven arg-guard arm, marketplaces-mode 'missing' fallback, failed-arm no-defect branch (coverage tail)
describe-plugin-compose.sh lacks a guard-class-2 grep pin
write-tier comment names only issue_comment trigger (confidence ~40, below threshold)
